all_violations.description: Test flow branches on runtime option counts, creating multi-path behavior that can hide deterministic failures.
all_violations.suggestion: Split each branch into explicit scenarios so each test has one deterministic path.

all_violations.description: Make-call flow branches on option availability instead of enforcing explicit fixture preconditions.
all_violations.suggestion: Use fixed fixture states or separate tests for "with options" and "without options" paths.

all_violations.description: Runtime timestamps are generated in signature tests; this is mostly safe but can reduce exact reproducibility in edge timing windows.
all_violations.suggestion: Prefer fixed timestamps for deterministic replay-oriented crypto/signature scenarios where feasible.

all_violations.description: Dynamic timestamp/number generation is used in integration tests, introducing slight nondeterministic variance.
all_violations.suggestion: Use deterministic seeded values in scenarios where uniqueness can be handled by fixture setup.

all_violations.description: Suite-level serial mode limits parallel safety and can mask inter-test coupling issues.
all_violations.suggestion: Remove serial mode and isolate shared state with per-test setup/cleanup.

all_violations.description: API automate suite is forced to serial execution, reducing confidence in isolation under parallel CI workers.
all_violations.suggestion: Run in parallel by default and retain serial only for proven hard dependencies.

all_violations.description: Tests mutate process-wide env flags, which can leak behavior across concurrently executed tests if scope expands.
all_violations.suggestion: Centralize env mutation in scoped helpers and ensure strict restoration per test block.

all_violations.description: Test file length is 350 lines, exceeding maintainability guidance for focused suites.
all_violations.suggestion: Split by behavior domain and extract common setup/helpers.

all_violations.description: Primary outbound flow is covered, but deeper failure-mode assertions (provider outages, refusal payload UX) are limited at E2E level.
all_violations.suggestion: Add explicit negative-flow E2E assertions for dispatch refusal and transient provider failure UX messaging.
all_violations.code_snippet: '[G2-AUTO-E2E-205][P1] inbox send-message and make-call actions launch neighbor workflows and submit outbound requests @P1',

all_violations.description: Actor-context boundary is validated, but additional malformed actor/header variants are not exhaustively covered in this suite.
all_violations.suggestion: Add malformed/partial actor-context permutations to tighten boundary regression detection.
all_violations.code_snippet: '[G2-AUTO-API-301][P0] mine bucket refuses reads when actor context is missing to preserve actor-owned queue isolation @P0',

all_violations.description: Responsive behavior is functionally asserted, but no explicit performance thresholds are asserted for breakpoint transitions.
all_violations.suggestion: Optionally add lightweight perf/timing assertions for key layout transitions in CI-safe ranges.

all_violations.description: Serial mode in E2E suite blocks parallel execution and increases CI wall-clock time.
all_violations.suggestion: Convert suite to parallel-safe isolation and remove serial mode where possible.

all_violations.description: Serial API suite reduces throughput despite mostly independent request-level scenarios.
all_violations.suggestion: Drop serial mode and rely on deterministic fixture setup per test case.

all_violations.description: Large integration suite repeatedly boots app/context and can become a runtime hotspot.
all_violations.suggestion: Extract shared app/bootstrap helpers and split suite for better shard distribution.

all_violations.description: Repeated full-login setup per E2E case increases runtime compared with cached storage-state patterns.
all_violations.suggestion: Adopt storage-state/session reuse where this does not reduce scenario independence.

top_10_recommendations.recommendation: Split oversized specs into narrower suites by capability.
Extract repeated request/header setup and correlation helpers.
Keep each file below 300 lines where practical for faster review and debugging.
Split conditional E2E branches into deterministic scenario-specific tests.
Use fixture-preconditioned data instead of branching on discovered UI option counts.
Prefer fixed timestamps for replay-sensitive signature validations where practical.
Eliminate blanket serial mode and validate suites under parallel workers.
Minimize process-global state mutation in route integration tests.
Keep explicit setup/teardown ownership near each mutated integration surface.
Add explicit negative E2E dispatch-refusal assertions.
